expectations.text: Review does NOT include a complexity/split advisory
expectations.evidence: The 140-line review contains no mention of complexity advisory or suggestion to split the PR. Appropriate for a 4-file bugfix.

expectations.text: Review does not flag breaking changes
expectations.evidence: No mention of 'breaking' anywhere in the review. All findings are about correctness, maintainability, and code quality.

The verdict section states: 'Request changes. Issue #1 is a functional bug: the routed block workspace element is missing #createViewContext and #provideViewContext calls for the root tab route... This should be fixed before merge.'
The review is 140 lines, under the 200-line threshold.

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues (formatting, whitespace, naming conventions, comment grammar, code style preferences) unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue -- it is a legitimate finding.
expectations.evidence: Issue 6 flags 'this._tabs?.length double-optional-chaining' as a 'Minor style nit' -- this is a purely cosmetic code style preference about optional chaining that has no impact on behavior, performance, or rendering.

eval_feedback.overall: Solid review with correct verdict and good bug identification. The one style nit (double optional chaining) is minor but triggers the no-stylistic-nitpicks assertion.
